title: The 175th edition of the Akutagawa and Naoki prizes is awarded to Chito Kosuna and Kasumi Asakura
summary: Japan's most prestigious literary awards recognize a new generation of writers.

title: Court releases video of prosecutor's interrogation who shouted 'do not underestimate the prosecution'
summary: The case involves a former Osaka prosecutor and reveals questionable practices during interrogations.

title: The bill to reform the Imperial House Law advances in the Diet amid intense debates
summary: Proposals on imperial succession and the role of the emperor divide the parties.

title: Lower House approves bill to establish a 'subcapital' in Japan
summary: The initiative aims to decentralize government functions but faces opposition criticism for being 'premature'.

title: Nurse arrested for introducing feces into a patient's IV drip, causing death
summary: The suspect, in charge of the night shift, denies the charges; the victim died from multiple organ failure.

title: Nichirei suffers cyberattack affecting restaurants and food manufacturers
summary: The logistics company confirms the cyberattack disrupted its systems, resuming operations on the 17th.

title: Prime Minister pushes debate on reducing consumption tax
summary: A possible reduced rate of 1% for basic foodstuffs is discussed, but the decision is delayed until August.

title: Obama-Kyoto route defined for Hokuriku Shinkansen extension
summary: The section between Katsuragawa and the Obama-Kyoto route will require 26 years of construction and generated expectations in local communities.

title: The Emperor and Empress and Princess Aiko enjoy their summer stay in Nasu
summary: The imperial family stays at the official Nasu residence to rest and enjoy nature.

title: Tokyo University Hospital to be restructured as 'Tokyo Hospital' from October
summary: The reorganization aims to improve governance and organizational culture after corruption scandals.

title: Trump withdraws controversial 20% toll in the Strait of Hormuz after pressure from allies
summary: The US administration shifts its strategy towards greater investment in the United States.

title: Spain beats France and advances to the World Cup final; England and Argentina face off in quarterfinals
summary: The Spanish team shows its defensive strength and reaches the final with solid play.

title: Incidents and emotions at the Nagoya sumo tournament: false fire alarm and key results
summary: Sumo sees intense days with accidental activation of an alarm during the entrance ceremony and notable matches.

title: Three former students ordered to pay compensation for death of a classmate under a mattress in Yamagata
summary: The ruling avoids statute of limitations after a third lawsuit and acknowledges full responsibility.

title: Third-grade boy dies hit by a truck at a pedestrian crossing in West Tokyo
summary: The traffic accident occurred when the minor was crossing with a green light and heading to school.

title: The Democratic Party for the People will hold its internal election on September 6
summary: Current leader Yuichiro Tamaki is expected to be reelected without major issues.

title: Life imprisonment confirmed for accused of murdering doctors in home visits with a shotgun
summary: Supreme Court rejects appeal and upholds the sentence of permanent reviewable prison.

title: FCC production company fined 250 million yen for rigging Olympic bids
summary: The company and its former executive receive sentences for manipulation in event contracting.

title: Independent bookstores close in Hong Kong due to ambiguity of national security laws
summary: The tightening of national security regulations causes the closure of multiple establishments.

title: Australia pushes law to regulate artificial intelligence and protect copyright
summary: The Prime Minister states that the use of data without permission is 'effective theft'.
